content:
# Matthew Chapter 14 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/MAT_014_page_001.jpg, MAT_014_page_002.jpg

### The Death of John the Baptist

1. Around that time, Herod the ruler heard the reports about Jesus.

2. He told his attendants, "This must be John the Baptist — he's come back from the dead! That's why he can do these miracles."

3. Herod had arrested John, chained him up, and thrown him in prison because of Herodias, his brother Philip's wife.

4. John had been telling Herod, "It is against God's law for you to marry her."

5. Herod wanted to kill John, but he was afraid of the people, who considered John a prophet.

6. But on Herod's birthday, the daughter of Herodias danced for the guests and delighted Herod.

7. He was so pleased that he swore an oath to give her anything she wanted.

8. Coached by her mother, the girl said, "I want the head of John the Baptist — right here, on a platter."

9. The king was distressed, but because he had made the oath in front of his dinner guests, he ordered it done.

10. He sent a guard to the prison to behead John.

11. The head was brought in on a platter and given to the girl, who carried it to her mother.

12. John's disciples came, took his body, and buried it. Then they went and told Jesus.

### Jesus Feeds Five Thousand

13. When Jesus heard the news, he slipped away by boat to a remote place to be alone. But the crowds found out and followed him on foot from the towns.

14. When he stepped ashore and saw the massive crowd, his heart went out to them, and he healed their sick.

15. As evening approached, the disciples came to him. "This is a remote place and it's getting late," they said. "Send the people away so they can go to the villages and buy food."

16. Jesus replied, "They don't need to leave. You feed them."

17. "All we have is five loaves of bread and two fish!" they protested.

18. "Bring them to me," he said.

19. He told the crowd to sit down on the grass. Then he took the five loaves and two fish, looked up to heaven, and gave thanks. He broke the bread and handed it to the disciples, who distributed it to the crowd.

20. Everyone ate and was satisfied. Afterward, the disciples picked up twelve baskets full of leftovers.

21. About five thousand men had eaten, not counting women and children.

### Jesus Walks on Water

22. Immediately afterward, Jesus insisted that his disciples get into the boat and cross to the other side ahead of him while he dismissed the crowd.

23. After sending the crowd away, he went up on the hillside alone to pray. When night fell, he was there by himself.

24. Meanwhile, the boat was far from shore, battered by the waves because the wind was blowing against it.

25. Between three and six in the morning, Jesus came toward them, walking on the lake.

26. When the disciples saw him walking on the water, they were terrified. "It's a ghost!" they screamed in fear.

27. But Jesus immediately called out, "Courage! It's me. Don't be afraid."

28. "Lord, if it's really you," Peter called back, "tell me to come to you on the water."

29. "Come," Jesus said. So Peter climbed out of the boat and walked on the water toward Jesus.

30. But when he noticed the fierce wind, he panicked. As he started to sink, he screamed, "Lord, save me!"

31. Instantly Jesus reached out and grabbed him. "You have so little faith," he said. "Why did you doubt?"

32. When they climbed back into the boat, the wind died down.

33. The men in the boat fell down before him. "You really are the Son of God!" they said.

### Healing at Gennesaret

34. They crossed the lake and landed at Gennesaret.

35. The people there recognized Jesus and sent word throughout the entire area. People brought all their sick to him

36. and begged him to let them just touch the edge of his robe. And everyone who touched it was healed.

---
## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

**Verse 13 — "he slipped away... to be alone":** Jesus has just learned that John — his cousin, his forerunner, the one who baptized him — has been murdered. He needs solitude to grieve. But the crowds find him anyway. What follows (v.14) is remarkable: despite his grief, "his heart went out to them." His compassion overrides his personal pain.

**Verse 19 — The four actions:** Took, blessed, broke, gave. These four verbs become the pattern for the Last Supper (26:26) and for Christian communion ever since. The feeding of the five thousand is not just a miracle of provision — it's a preview of the meal that will define the new covenant.

**Verse 27 — "It's me" (ἐγώ εἰμι):** On the surface, Jesus is simply identifying himself. But the Greek ἐγώ εἰμι is the same phrase used for God's name in Exodus 3:14 ("I AM"). Jesus is walking on the sea — something only God does (Job 9:8, Psalm 77:19). He arrives in the darkest hour. He speaks the divine name. This is a theophany — God showing up in person.

**Verses 28-31 — Peter walks on water:** Only Matthew tells this story. Peter is the disciple who dares to step out — and the disciple who sinks. The pattern is faith → fear → rescue. Jesus doesn't let him drown. The hand is always extended. "Why did you doubt?" is not condemnation — it's an invitation to deeper trust.

— PNEUMA, AI Translator
